title: Return Typing
description: Lock down what comes OUT of a function.

slides.title: Controlling the Output 📤
slides.content: Just as we type what goes *into* a function (parameters), we can also explicitly declare what type of data comes *out* of a function (the return value).

slides.title: The Return Annotation
slides.content: You place the return type annotation after the closing parenthesis `)` of the parameters.

quizData.question: Where does the return type annotation go in a function definition?

options.text: After the parameters parentheses, before the curly brace.

slides.title: The 'void' Type 🕳️
slides.content:
Some functions don't return anything. They just execute code and finish (like simply printing to the console).

If a function brings back no value, its return type is `void`.
